commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `inspectPortUsages()` for batched Unix listener snapshots, wires daemon status to use it for distinct daemon/CLI ports, exports the helper, and adds focused regression tests.

PR surface: Source +132, Tests +104. Total +236 across 5 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the PR-introduced command-scope regression by source inspection: `inspectPortUsage(port)` reaches the all-LISTEN lsof snapshot on the PR head, while restart-health still calls `inspectPortUsage` one port at a time. I did not live-reproduce the disruptive launchservicesd/WindowServer saturation scenario.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] A narrow repair can preserve the contributor's batch helper while restoring targeted single-port lsof behavior and adding command-shape coverage.

target.title: fix(ports): batch macOS lsof listener inspection to one spawn per cycle
target.body:
## Summary

Refs #90548. This is a partial mitigation and intentionally does not close the issue.

This changes Unix listener inspection from one `lsof` spawn per inspected port to one LISTEN snapshot per same-cycle multi-port inspection, then filters the snapshot back into the existing per-port `PortUsage` shape.

## Affected Surface

- `src/infra/ports-inspect.ts`
  - `readUnixListenerSnapshot`
  - `inspectPortUsages`
  - `recordsForPort`
- `src/infra/ports.ts`
  - exports `inspectPortUsages`
- `src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.ts`
  - batches daemon/CLI port status inspection when the two ports differ

## Scope

Changed:

- Unix LISTEN inspection now supports a shared snapshot command:
  - `lsof -nP -iTCP -sTCP:LISTEN -FpFcn`
- The snapshot parser preserves pid, command, address, and per-port filtered detail.
- `inspectDaemonPortStatuses` uses the batch helper for daemon/CLI same-cycle checks.
- `checkPortInUse` still runs per port when no matching listener is found.

Intentionally not changed:

- Windows netstat inspection remains separate.
- Linux `ss` fallback remains separate and per-port when lsof fails.
- `inspectPortConnections` / ESTABLISHED connection inspection is untouched.
- The public per-port `PortUsage` result shape is preserved.
- Per-port `detail` does not include unrelated sockets from the all-port lsof snapshot.
- Restart-health polling remains outside this PR, so #90548 should stay open for that remaining surface.

## Real Behavior Proof

### behavior

macOS Unix listener inspection previously spawned `lsof` once per inspected port. The patched multi-port helper should inspect multiple same-cycle listener ports with one all-LISTEN `lsof` snapshot and still return filtered per-port `PortUsage` results. `gateway status` wiring is covered separately by the status collector regression test that calls `inspectPortUsages([daemonPort, cliPort])` when daemon and CLI ports differ.

### environment

- Machine: local Mac mini4 development machine
- Platform: `darwin`
- Checkout branch: `wolf/ports-inspect-batch-lsof-listeners`
- Head tested: `edd01c72ae9d8b8fef694f700a4161382ce6cf8b`
- Real process path: Node `child_process.spawn` was instrumented only to log `/usr/sbin/lsof` invocations; the hook forwarded to the real `/usr/sbin/lsof` process.
- Live listeners: two temporary local TCP listeners bound to `127.0.0.1` on ephemeral ports.
- No secrets, production services, or private traffic were used.

### steps

```sh
# Live macOS behavior proof: start two local TCP listeners, call the real branch helper,
# log child_process.spawn calls for /usr/sbin/lsof, and still execute the real lsof binary.
OPENCLAW_LSOF_LOG="$tmpdir/lsof.log" node --import "$tmpdir/hook.mjs" --import tsx --input-type=module

# Focused regression tests and checks on the same head.
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/infra/ports.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts
./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/infra/ports-inspect.ts src/infra/ports.ts src/infra/ports.test.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.ts src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts
git diff --check origin/main..HEAD
gitleaks git --redact --log-opts origin/main..HEAD
```

### evidence

Live macOS proof output:

```json
{
  "platform": "darwin",
  "head": "edd01c72ae9d8b8fef694f700a4161382ce6cf8b",
  "proofKind": "live macOS child_process spawn hook; real /usr/sbin/lsof still executed",
  "ports": [49698, 49699],
  "lsofCallCount": 1,
  "lsofCalls": [
    {
      "command": "/usr/sbin/lsof",
      "args": ["-nP", "-iTCP", "-sTCP:LISTEN", "-FpFcn"]
    }
  ],
  "results": [
    {
      "port": 49698,
      "status": "busy",
      "listenerCount": 1,
      "listenerAddresses": ["127.0.0.1:49698"],
      "detailMentionsRequestedPort": true
    },
    {
      "port": 49699,
      "status": "busy",
      "listenerCount": 1,
      "listenerAddresses": ["127.0.0.1:49699"],
      "detailMentionsRequestedPort": true
    }
  ]
}
```

Focused validation:

- `src/infra/ports.test.ts`: 14/14 passed.
- `src/cli/daemon-cli/status.gather.test.ts`: 29/29 passed.
- Targeted `oxfmt --check`: all 5 matched files use correct format.
- `git diff --check origin/main..HEAD`: passed.
- `gitleaks git --redact --log-opts origin/main..HEAD`: no leaks found.

The deterministic spawn-count regression still asserts exactly one mocked `lsof` invocation for ports `[18789, 19001]`, verifies that the args end with `-nP -iTCP -sTCP:LISTEN -FpFcn`, and verifies per-port filtering excludes an unrelated port `3000` listener from requested-port details.

The status collector regression verifies daemon/CLI port status inspection calls `inspectPortUsages([19001, 18789])` and does not call the per-port `inspectPortUsage` path when the ports differ.

### observedResult

On the live macOS proof run, `inspectPortUsages([49698, 49699])` executed exactly one real `/usr/sbin/lsof` process with all-LISTEN args and returned `busy` listener results for both requested ports, with each per-port detail mentioning only the requested listener port. Focused tests also confirmed the changed daemon status path uses the batch helper for distinct daemon/CLI ports.

### notTested

- I did not reproduce launchservicesd / WindowServer saturation. That scenario is disruptive and risks destabilizing the desktop session.
- I did not start or mutate a real OpenClaw LaunchAgent service.
- I did not cover restart-health polling in this PR. Because that remaining repeated health-loop surface is still open, this PR now uses `Refs #90548` rather than a closing reference.

## De-duplication Evidence

Immediately before the original push, these five independent open-PR searches returned no results:

- `Fixes #90548`
- `#90548`
- `ports-inspect lsof iTCP`
- `inspectPortUsage readUnixListeners`
- `launchservicesd lsof port`

## Narrow Fix Definition

1. This is a shared helper/default-policy gap: the Unix port-inspection helper owned a per-port process-spawn strategy.
2. Fixing only one visible caller would leave adjacent status/diagnostic paths able to repeat the same per-port lsof pattern, so the fix is helper-level plus batch-aware status caller wiring.
3. Maintainers should expect a canonical shared helper fix rather than a local status-only patch because the bug is process-spawn strategy, not output formatting.
4. Supersession risk is lower with this shared boundary: a narrower macOS-app-only or status-only patch would be more likely to be replaced.
target.author: luoyanglang

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
